title: Restrict agent aliases to pre-registered BeadHub roles
description:
## Problem

Agents have been spinning up with ad-hoc random aliases (e.g. worker-fj7o, attach-dev) that aren't pre-registered in BeadHub. This causes:
- Lost chat memory — new aliases have no history
- Coordination gaps — orchestrator can't track unregistered agents
- Identity sprawl — dozens of dead aliases accumulate on the server

## Standard Agent Team

The following three agents are the fixed team across ALL BeadHub projects:

| Alias | Role | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| **ordis** | coordinator | Global orchestrator (Remote Control pod). Plans, assigns, reviews, unblocks. Reachable via Discord and Claude Code Remote Control. |
| **neo** | developer | Worker agent. Implements features, fixes bugs, submits PRs. |
| **hawk** | reviewer | QA/review agent. Reviews PRs, checks security, test coverage, code quality. |

### Per-project registration

Agents are scoped per-project in BeadHub. When setting up a new project, register all three:

```bash
bdh :init --alias ordis --role coordinator   # from the main repo
bdh :add-worktree developer --alias neo      # creates worktree + registers
bdh :add-worktree reviewer --alias hawk      # creates worktree + registers
```

This ensures every project has the same team with consistent aliases, roles, and chat history.

### Rules
- Agents must ONLY assume one of the three pre-registered aliases (ordis, neo, hawk)
- No ad-hoc aliases — no worker-xyz, no attach-dev, no task-specific names
- Worker K8s Jobs must claim `neo` or `hawk` via `bdh :init --alias <name>`, not create new identities
- If a project needs more workers, register them with the human first (e.g. adding a fourth agent)

### Global orchestrator = coordinator per project
- ordis (the Remote Control pod) assumes the coordinator role for each project
- When ordis does `bdh :init` into a project, it becomes that project's coordinator
- The bdh notify hook fires on every bdh command, so ordis automatically receives messages from neo and hawk
- ordis is reachable from both Discord (via discord-bridge) and Claude Code Remote Control

### Cleanup done
- Deleted all old ad-hoc aliases: alice, bob, charlie, attach-dev, bridge-dev, test-dev, worker-fj7o
- Registered ordis (coordinator), neo (developer), hawk (reviewer) for hq-beadhub
- Updated all policy playbooks to reference ordis by alias

### Remaining work
- Update orchestrator pod entrypoint to use `--alias ordis` (see beadhub-4j4)
- Update policy to enforce "no ad-hoc aliases" as an invariant
- Document the bootstrap procedure for new projects
close_reason: Implemented: CLAUDE.md rules, policy invariants, homelab-k8s ConfigMap/entrypoint updated, old agents deleted, ordis/neo/hawk registered

title: Update orchestrator pod entrypoint to use alias ordis
description:
## Context

The orchestrator alias has been renamed from "orchestrator" to "ordis" in BeadHub. The orchestrator pod on the K8s cluster (managed by ArgoCD in homelab-k8s) still uses the old alias.

## Requirements

1. Update the orchestrator entrypoint in `Woody88/homelab-k8s` to use `bdh :init --alias ordis --role coordinator`
2. Ensure the ConfigMap or CLAUDE.md mounted in the pod reflects the new alias
3. The pod must register as "ordis" so Discord messages routed to the coordinator land on the right alias
4. Verify the Remote Control session works after the rename

## Important
- This change must go through a commit to homelab-k8s (ArgoCD syncs from Git)
- Do NOT kubectl patch/apply the orchestrator Deployment directly
close_reason: Implemented: CLAUDE.md rules, policy invariants, homelab-k8s ConfigMap/entrypoint updated, old agents deleted, ordis/neo/hawk registered

title: Ordis Control Plane Architecture
description:
# Ordis Control Plane Architecture

## Overview

Ordis (coordinator) runs as a project-agnostic control plane that receives messages 
via Discord, processes them with `claude -p`, and responds back through Discord. 
No Claude Remote Control needed.

## Components

### 1. Central Control-Plane Project (BeadHub)
- **Project slug:** `control-plane`
- **Project ID:** `57dc7ce5-4722-480e-a92c-a29b50ef41bb`
- **Ordis agent_id:** `953d4875-f5c7-492f-971b-0918222601b1`
- **API key:** stored in Bitwarden
- All Discord→ordis messages land here regardless of which project they're about
- Project-agnostic: ordis can work on any repo from this base

### 2. Discord Interface
- **Channel:** `#ordis` (ID: `1478072914543775824`)
- **Webhook:** configured for the ordis channel
- Single channel for all communication with ordis
- User sends messages, sees real-time tool activity, gets responses
- No Remote Control needed — Discord is the full interface

### 3. Discord Bridge (existing, minor changes)
- Saves Discord messages from `#ordis` channel to BeadHub chat in the control-plane project
- Picks up ordis responses from BeadHub → posts to Discord channel
- Uses control-plane project API key (not hq-beadhub key)

### 4. Orchestrator Pod (K8s)

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  message-watcher (main container)           │
│  - bdh :init for control-plane project      │
│  - loops: bdh :aweb chat pending            │
│  - kicks claude -p --resume when pending    │
│  - claude processes, hooks handle the rest  │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  (no sidecar needed — setup token lasts 1y) │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

- Uses `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` from `claude setup-token` (1 year validity)
- No credentials.json, no oauth-refresh sidecar, no init container needed
- Single container architecture

### 5. Claude Code Hooks (settings.json)

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "PostToolUse": [
      {
        "hooks": [{"command": "bdh :notify", "type": "command"}],
        "matcher": ".*"
      },
      {
        "hooks": [{"command": "/scripts/discord-status.sh", "type": "command"}],
        "matcher": ".*"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

- `bdh :notify` — picks up new BeadHub chat messages mid-task (fires after every tool use)
- `discord-status.sh` — posts tool activity to Discord channel (deterministic, 100% reliable)
- Both are static shell commands, not dependent on Claude behavior

### 6. Authentication
- **Claude auth:** `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` env var from `claude setup-token` (1 year, no refresh needed)
- **BeadHub auth:** HMAC internal auth secret (cross-project admin access)
- **Discord:** webhook URL for posting, bot token for thread management

## Message Flow

```
User posts in #ordis Discord channel
       ↓
discord-bridge saves to BeadHub chat (control-plane project)
       ↓
message-watcher detects pending message (bdh :aweb chat pending)
       ↓
kicks: claude -p "Check pending messages" --resume <session>
       ↓
Claude starts working, uses tools
       ↓  (after each tool use)
       ├── bdh :notify → catches any new messages arriving mid-task
       └── discord-status.sh → posts tool activity to Discord
       ↓
Claude responds via bdh :aweb chat
       ↓
discord-bridge picks up response → posts to #ordis channel
       ↓
User sees response + real-time activity in Discord
```

## Multi-Project Support

- Control-plane project is ordis's "inbox" — always active
- When user says "check CI for hq-beadhub" → ordis clones/cd's into that repo
- When user says "review PR on project-X" → ordis switches to that repo
- All communication stays in the control-plane project's chat
- ordis can run `bdh :init` for specific projects when needed for coordination

## Key Design Decisions

1. **No Remote Control** — RC is preview, unreliable, and can't receive push messages. 
   Discord provides better visibility (persistent messages vs ephemeral typing indicator).
2. **Setup token over credentials.json** — 1 year validity, no refresh sidecar needed. 
   Setup token works for `claude -p` (doesn't need RC scope).
3. **`bdh :notify` as the message bus** — existing BeadHub protocol, no custom Redis queues.
   Fires on every tool use during active Claude sessions.
4. **Static hooks for visibility** — typing indicator and status updates are deterministic 
   shell commands, not dependent on Claude choosing to post updates.
5. **Thin message watcher** — only detects "something pending" and kicks `claude -p`. 
   All message reading/routing handled by `bdh :notify` inside the Claude session.

## Infrastructure

| Component | Image | Notes |
|-----------|-------|-------|
| message-watcher | ghcr.io/woody88/claude-agent:latest | Main container, runs watcher + claude -p |
| discord-bridge | ghcr.io/woody88/discord-bridge:latest | Existing, needs config update for control-plane |

## Secrets (Bitwarden)

| Secret | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| claude-setup-token | CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN for claude -p (1 year) |
| control-plane-api-key | BeadHub API key for control-plane project |
| beadhub-internal-auth | HMAC secret for cross-project admin access |
| discord-webhook-ordis | Webhook URL for #ordis channel |
close_reason: Implemented: orchestrator runs message-watcher + claude -p (not RC), discord-bridge routes #ordis channel to control-plane project, typing indicators, discord-status.sh hook. Deployed to K8s via ArgoCD.

title: Route bdh chat to global orchestrator via bridge relay
description:
## Problem

The global orchestrator (K8s BLPOP dispatcher) can't receive bdh chat messages from developer agents. When a developer sends `bdh :aweb chat send-and-wait orchestrator 'question'`, the message goes to BeadHub DB and Redis pub/sub, but nothing pushes it to `orchestrator:inbox`. The developer's send-and-wait times out.

## Solution

1. **Bridge relay**: discord-bridge detects chat events targeting 'orchestrator' and pushes them to `orchestrator:inbox`
2. **Stateless chat handling**: Dispatcher runs fresh `claude -p` for each chat (not --resume). Context recovered from bdh (chat history, status, bead details)
3. **Role enforcement**: Orchestrator always uses coordinator role via bdh :init; spawned agents use roles defined in bdh policy
4. **Project context**: Bridge includes repo info from BeadHub API so orchestrator can clone and set up project context
5. **Cleanup**: Delete broken orchestrator-listener Deployment

## Repos touched
- hq-beadhub: discord-bridge changes (redis-listener, types, config, beadhub-client)
- homelab-k8s: orchestrator.yaml dispatcher + orchestrator-docs.yaml CLAUDE.md

## Plan file
See /home/woodson/.claude/plans/purring-hopping-hanrahan.md
close_reason: Implemented chat-to-orchestrator relay in discord-bridge, added processChatMessage handler in dispatcher, updated orchestrator docs with chat handling/role enforcement/project querying, fixed worker-template role placeholder, deleted broken orchestrator-listener

title: Enforce server-only ticket sync — no local dolt DB
description:
## Problem

Agents have been attempting to use local dolt databases for ticket operations, which fail with 'table not found: issues' errors (see bridge-dev's mail). Agents also risk syncing upstream fork tickets into the project.

## Requirements

### No local dolt
- Agents must NEVER start or depend on a local dolt database for beads/ticket operations
- All ticket operations go through `bdh` (BeadHub server API) or `bd` (git-backed JSONL)
- If `bd list` or `bd show` fails, the agent should NOT try to fix dolt — it should use `bdh list` / `bdh show` instead

### Ticket creation sync flow
When an agent creates a new ticket:
1. `bd create ...` (creates locally in git-backed JSONL)
2. `bd sync` (pushes to git remote)
3. `bdh sync` or equivalent server sync (ensures BeadHub server picks up the new ticket from git)

Git is the source of truth. The server syncs FROM git, not the other way around.

### No upstream contamination
- This repo is a fork of beadhub/beadhub but the upstream remote has been removed
- Agents must NEVER add an upstream remote
- Agents must NEVER sync tickets from the upstream fork
- Only tickets created in Woody88/hq-beadhub are valid
- If upstream tickets appear in the server DB, they must be purged

### Policy update needed
- Add these rules to the project policy via `bdh :policy`
- Add to CLAUDE.md under a "Ticket Sync Rules" section
- Update the orchestrator system prompt in homelab-k8s
close_reason: Implemented: CLAUDE.md rules, policy invariants, homelab-k8s ConfigMap/entrypoint updated, old agents deleted, ordis/neo/hawk registered

title: Update orchestrator system prompt for project context switching via bdh
description: The orchestrator should be able to bdh :init into any project and inherit full chat history. Update the system prompt to: (1) explain project switching, (2) guide brainstorm-to-project transitions, (3) clarify when to use ephemeral sessions vs bdh-backed persistence. Also update known projects list as new projects are added.
close_reason: Completed by hand — system prompt already updated in homelab-k8s. Orchestrator incorrectly rewrote its own Deployment spec when attempting this task.

title: Add SSH access to orchestrator pod for ADB/emulator on desktop
description: The orchestrator needs SSH access to the human's desktop machine to run ADB commands for headless Android builds. Requires: SSH key pair, network route from Pi 5 to desktop, ADB CLI in agent image. Enables: build -> screenshot verify -> loop workflow.

title: CI/CD: Auto-build and push Docker images on merge
description:
Currently building and pushing Docker images is manual: pull changes, run docker buildx, push to ghcr.io, then restart pods. This needs automation.

Images:
- ghcr.io/woody88/discord-bridge:main (from discord-bridge/)
- ghcr.io/woody88/beadhub:main (from ./)
- ghcr.io/woody88/claude-agent:latest (from agent-image/)

Repo: Woody88/hq-beadhub

Requirements:
- GitHub Actions workflow that triggers on merge to main
- Detect which Dockerfile contexts changed and only build those
- Build for linux/arm64 (Pi 5 target)
- Push to ghcr.io
- Optionally trigger pod restart on the cluster (webhook or ArgoCD image updater)

Considerations:
- arm64 builds are slow on GitHub Actions (QEMU emulation) — may need self-hosted runner or buildx cloud
- ArgoCD Image Updater could watch ghcr.io tags and auto-update deployments

title: Remove sitelink-orchestrator CronJob
description: The general orchestrator Deployment replaces the project-scoped sitelink-orchestrator CronJob. Remove sitelink-orchestrator.yaml from homelab-k8s/manifests/platform/beadhub/ and update kustomization.yaml. The general orchestrator can bdh :init into any project on demand.

title: Rebuild agent image with ADB CLI
description: Add ADB (Android Debug Bridge) CLI to the claude-agent Docker image (ghcr.io/woody88/claude-agent:latest). Required for headless Android builds and screenshot verification loop. Rebuild and push for arm64.

title: Add Discord attachment support to orchestrator→bridge pipeline
description:
Enable the orchestrator agent to send file attachments (screenshots, audio, etc.) to Discord threads.

## Repo
Clone Woody88/hq-beadhub — the discord-bridge source is in discord-bridge/src/.

## Current State
The pipeline is text-only: Claude stdout → Redis outbox (JSON with response string) → bridge posts text via Discord webhook.

## Required Changes

1. **Outbox message format** (types.ts): Add optional `attachments` field to OrchestratorOutboxMessage:
   - Each attachment: `{ filename: string, path: string }` (file path on orchestrator pod)
   - Alternative: base64-encoded content if files can't be shared via filesystem

2. **Agent file convention**: Claude saves files to a known directory (e.g. /tmp/discord-attachments/) and the dispatcher includes them in the outbox message. The dispatcher script in the orchestrator Deployment manifest (homelab-k8s repo) needs to scan for attachments after Claude exits.

3. **Bridge relay** (orchestrator-relay.ts): When outbox message has attachments, use `webhook.send({ content, files: [...] })` instead of plain text. Discord.js supports AttachmentBuilder for file uploads.

4. **Cross-pod file transfer**: Since orchestrator and bridge run in different pods, files can't be shared via filesystem. Options:
   - Base64-encode files in the Redis message (simplest, works for <8MB)
   - Shared PVC mount (more complex)
   - Temporary presigned URL (overkill)

## HIL (Human-in-the-Loop)
After implementation, the worker MUST ask the human to verify in Discord that attachments display correctly before closing this bead. Send a test screenshot and a test text file to confirm.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Orchestrator can send image files (png/jpg) to Discord threads
- [ ] Orchestrator can send text/log files to Discord threads  
- [ ] Messages with attachments also include text content
- [ ] Files >8MB are gracefully rejected with an error message
- [ ] Human confirms attachments render correctly in Discord
## Status Note (2026-03-01)

Code is implemented but attachment delivery to Discord threads is broken.

**Bug:** Webhook file uploads to threads fail with Discord error 10003 (Unknown Channel).
- Text-only messages post to threads successfully via webhook
- When files are included, Discord rejects the request with "Unknown Channel"
- Root cause: Discord enforces stricter channel validation for multipart (file) webhook requests vs JSON text requests
- Affected path: sendAsAgent() in discord-sender.ts -> webhook.send({ files, threadId })

**Fix needed:** Send files via bot client directly rather than via webhook when targeting threads.
## Status Note (2026-03-01)

Code is implemented but attachment delivery to Discord threads is broken.

**Bug:** Webhook file uploads to threads fail with Discord error 10003 (Unknown Channel).
- Text-only messages post to threads successfully via webhook
- When files are included, Discord rejects the request with "Unknown Channel"
- Root cause: Discord enforces stricter channel validation for multipart (file) webhook requests vs JSON text requests
- Affected path: sendAsAgent() in discord-sender.ts -> webhook.send({ files, threadId })

**Fix needed:** Send files via bot client directly rather than via webhook when targeting threads.

title: Add Cloudflare access to orchestrator pod
description: Give the orchestrator access to Cloudflare for tunnel management and DNS operations. Requires adding Cloudflare API token as a K8s secret and mounting it in the orchestrator Deployment.
